Eye gods! What now" cried the jur-
ors as they filed into the upper corri-
doris of the courthouse this morning.


The benches from Judge Richardson 9
court room to the stairway were piled
high with millinery creations, some of
them created some time ago, some
really prehistoric. Besides the hats
of many colors and shapes were coats,
capes, and jackets, all parts of fem
mime attire. "Have the ladies' come
over to take in the court house? Is
the w. G. 7. U. convention to meet
here? What is it? Who are they? All
these questions followed one another
in rapid successin.


Ont worry, gentlemen" said the
populist party John Coffeen) as he
approached them. "This does not hap
pen very often, but today happens to
be examination day for those who think
they would make good teachers"


The party was right. 1t is examina-
ton day in the county superintendent
Office, There were only lao men, wo-
men and children who reported for
duty this morning. They filed the
superintendent room and offices to
overhowing. Those who could not find
seats in these rooms were taken into
Judge Remnants court room and cham
bers. On the bench where Judge Ken
